description_long: This is a wooden crate marked with 'ATLAS POWDER CO' and 'TNT', suggesting it was used for transporting or storing explosives. The box is constructed from planks of wood, reinforced with diagonal and horizontal slats forming an 'X' pattern on each face. The wood appears weathered and unfinished, giving it a rugged, utilitarian look. The top is flat and solid, matching the sides in construction, and there are no visible handles or locks. The crate is cubic in shape, with all sides of similar dimensions, and the text labeling is only present on one face. Its design is typical of storage boxes used in industrial or historical urban settings, such as warehouses, garages, or construction sites.
description: A weathered wooden crate with 'ATLAS POWDER CO' and 'TNT' markings, reinforced with diagonal slats and a flat top. The box has a rugged, utilitarian appearance and no visible handles or locks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the 'ATLAS POWDER CO', 'TNT', and 'L.C.C. 218' markings, with prominent diagonal and horizontal wooden slats.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain wooden slats in an 'X' pattern, with no text or markings visible.
description_view_2: Another side view with the same 'X' slat pattern, slightly rotated, and no visible text.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the 'X' slat pattern, similar to the other sides, with no text or markings.
